rows.title: Regional capacity building for North Korea sanctions implementation
rows.description: This project strengthens the implementation of UN Security Council Resolution (UNSCR) sanctions on North Korea in at-risk regions on counter-proliferation finance, compliance, and maritime anti-smuggling. Project activities include: convening training workshops on North Korean sanctions implementation for government and private sector experts from states in, Central America, Central Asia, Francophone Africa, Latin America, and Southeast Asia.

rows.reporting: Foreign Affairs, Trade and Development Canada (DFATD)
rows.title: Including Women and Victims of Gender-Based Violence in Transitional Justice Processes
rows.description: This project works with women and other surviors of gender-based violence in Nepal, Sri Lanka, Syria and Tunisia to strengthen their ability to effectively engage in transitional justice  measures. The project also aims to ensure that states and state institutions take a gendered approach in the planning, designing and implementing of transitional justice measures. The project also works to undertake international outreach to enhance and build norms around the gendered dimensions of human rights violations and transitional justice. Project activities include: (1) conducting preparatory meetings, such as gender and transitional justice sessions, with survivors; (2) capacity building with women’s groups; and (3) producing reports and newsletters on the outcomes.

rows.reporting: Foreign Affairs, Trade and Development Canada (DFATD)
rows.title: Promoting the Ratification & Implementation of International Legal Frameworks for Nuclear Security
rows.description: This project supports the work of the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime in promoting the ratification and implementation of international legal frameworks against nuclear terrorism globally. Project activities include hosting an international workshop on the universalization of the International Convention for the Suppression of Acts of Nuclear Terrorism, the Convention on the Physical Protection of Nuclear Material and its 2005 Amendment.

rows.reporting: Foreign Affairs, Trade and Development Canada (DFATD)
rows.title: Canada Fund for Local Initiatives (CFLI)  - DELHI - 2018 to 2019
rows.description: The Canada Fund for Local Initiatives (CFLI) supports small projects proposed and implemented by local organizations in Bhutan, India and Nepal. The CFLI contributes to the overall goal of reducing poverty by providing funding for small projects that offer direct social, economic or technical assistance to local populations.  The CFLI retains the same broad themes set out since 2016, which align closely with the 6 pillars of the Feminist International Assistance Policy. This allows flexibility to respond to and focus on themes relevant to local needs. The six thematic priorities include: (1) contributing to inclusive governance, including diversity and LGBTQ2, democracy, human rights and the rule of law; (2) contributing to gender equality and the empowerment of women and girls; (3) contributing to peace and security, with a focus on conflict prevention and building peace; (4) contributing to growth that works for everyone, including women’s economic rights, decent jobs and entrepreneurship, investing in the poorest and most vulnerable, and safeguarding economic gains; (5) contributing to environment and climate action, focusing on adaptation, mitigation and  water management; and (6) contributing to human dignity, covering health, education and nutrition.

rows.reporting: Foreign Affairs, Trade and Development Canada (DFATD)
rows.title: Reducing Unemployment Among People with Disabilities
rows.description: This project aims to help 10,000 women and men with disabilities to find sustainable employment and increase their household incomes. The project seeks to identify and address the key causes of chronic unemployment, underemployment, and poverty of women and men with disabilities in Dhaka, Bangladesh; Hyderabad, India; and Kathmandu, Nepal. It focuses on three sectors that employ a proportionally large number of people with disabilities: cottage or home-based industries, the hospitality sector, and food processing. The project seeks to influence small and medium enterprises to hire and promote people with disabilities, as well as increase the employability of persones with disabilities. It supports 300 employers in offering work placements for people with disabilities, thereby providing positive models for other employers in the target countries.

rows.reporting: Gates Foundation
rows.title: Technical Assistance to Support Early Adoption of Reinvented Toilet
rows.description: to support accelerated uptake of improved sanitation technologies in low income countries

rows.reporting: Gates Foundation
rows.title: COVID-19: Nepal Response
rows.description: to roll-out an evidence-backed mask wearing campaign in Nepal of COVID-19: Nepal Response from 06/28/2021 to 12/31/2021. Grantee name: Environment and Public Health Organization (ENPHO).

rows.reporting: Gates Foundation
rows.title: Dissemination of Vaccine Exemplars in Global Health
rows.description: to disseminate findings from vaccine exemplars research work, and to ensure uptake of research by relevant stakeholders

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Sustainable Livelihood and Child Development Project
rows.description:
The project has been implemented to improve livelihood of poor families in project area communities, where most of the community people are dependent on traditional agriculture. Even though they have enough natural resources, they are not being able to uti
lize them due to lack of skills and appropriate technology. The project has provided training on livelihood area e.g. appropriate farming and livestock. The project has also worked together with schools by giving trainings to teachers and providing support
 for classroom management. This has been done in child sponsorship are in Chitwan but also in Okhaldunga's working area, and there are together 13 Kids' clubs. The project goal is improved quality of life among the vulnerable people. Project purpose is imp
roved livelihood of 450 families in Khijikati, Khijifalate, khijichandeshwori, Ragani and Pokali VDCs of Okhaldhunga district and Padampur VDC of Chitwan district. Fida’s partner in the project is a local NGO, Rescue Nepal. The project results in increas
ed livestock production, appropriate farming methods, fruit farming and income generating activities. Teachers and school management committees are provided with information on child friendly education and kid’s clubs are actively functioning. The projec
t benefits children, adults and ethnic group farmers.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Maithili Empowerment Programme
rows.description:
Maithili communities are the main beneficiary groups. They need to be empowered and supported to develop their skills and knowledge to be completely self-dependent in fulfilling the basic needs. The project has provided improved access to child friendly pr
imary education by trained teachers and good learning facilities, arranged literacy classes to underprivileged community members and skills training to youth. It has trained communities to take ownership of their own development and apply for benefits from
 the government. It has raised awareness on sanitation, hygiene and preventive health care e.g. through CHE-groups and radio programs. Project goals is improved quality of life among the vulnerable people in the region. The purpose is improved quality of l
ife in Maithili communities. Project results in access and quality of education in the working area. The communities' capacity and ownership of the community development is improves. The awareness and knowledge on health, sanitation and hygiene has improve
s significantly. Beneficiaries include Maithili communities as a whole, but especially women and children. In 2016, youth will benefit more than earlier. Fida partners with Relative Nepal in the project

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Youth Centre Programme
rows.description:
Unemployment is a big problem in Nepal and especially it concerns the youth in the country. The project supports youth in skills improvement and identification of income ideas. Also free time activities are provided to create meaningful ways to spend time.
 This project works with youth in Nepal, helping them to improve their skills and to get a job. Youth empowerment has been done through the career and counseling programs and free-time activities. Project goal is improved quality of life among vulnerable p
eople. The purpose is increased employment opportunities of youth in the community of the Banke and Nuwakot districts. Fida partners with a local NGO, Nepal Integral Mission Society (NIMS). As a result, the youth are more equipped in finding jobs as their 
skill level has improved and they have received career counselling. The youth also utilize their free time on more productive activities than before.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: CMC-N School Mental Health Program/Children
rows.description: Developing and advocating for the mental health care an child-friendly teaching in schools in Salyan and Kailali.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Support to Nepal's School Sector Reform Programme
rows.description:
Nepal has requested the Development Partners to support the education sector through the School Sector Reform Program (SSRP). The overall objective of the SSRP is to contribute towards poverty reduction through inclusive responsive and accountable local go
vernance and participatory community-led development. The Program development objective is to increase access to and quality of school education especially for children from marginalized groups. There are seven development partners in the Joint Financial A
greement with the Ministry of Education of Nepal. Finnish participation to the Joint Financing Agreement is ? 16 million during 2009-2014 and the additional financing ? 70 million during 2014-2016.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Promoting Child Rights through Educational Settings
rows.description:
Statistics shows that more than 60% of the children in community schools of Kathmandu are child domestic workers and those in Kabhre one of the major supply side are vulnerable to separation. Teachers still have hangover of traditional teaching practice de
spite effort from the government and CSOs. Right to protection of these children is violated or at risk. Making the education system child friendly and improving the quality of education helps to secure children?s right to protection and education as funda
mental aspects of child right. The overall objective of the project is promoting child friendly schooling and enhancing quality education in four districts of Nepal: Kathmandu Bhaktapur Lalitpur and Kabhre. The project will promote previously formed struct
ures; however reformation will be done to revitalize the community structures and mechanisms as per need.The project works to promote right to protection of the children in community school of Kathmandu and Kabhre districts. This project will directly bene
fit the following: Community Schools Teachers Community people; member of Parent Teacher Associations (PTA) School Management Committees (SMC) Village Education Committees (VEC) and Child Protection Committees (CPC) children and child clubs; other local go
vernment authorities and political leaders.Following activities will be carried out to achieve the project goal:1 Formation reformation capacity development and mobilization of local child protection and education related mechanisms like PTA SMC CPC CC and
 VEC2 Capacity development of teachers3 Promotion of value education among the children4 Material support to the needy children for reducing the vulnerability of drop out5 Basic infrastructure support to the needy schools6 Publication of different ICE mate
rials7 Identification of legal and policy gaps and lobby with governmentThe project makes mandatory provision 50% participation of women in decision making level of the community mechanisms and promotion of girls? education. Empowerment of women and margin
alized sector of community; women and children will enhance their bargaining capacity and help to promote their overall human right situation; it also help to reduce climate vulnerability with access to information and resources after being empowered. Gend
er equality and climate sustainability are taught in the child clubs.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Supporting Democracy through Digital Investigative Journalism in Nepal
rows.description:
This continuation project has its former partner organisation and a new content digital journalism. The immediate objective of this project is to impart digital investigative journalism skills to journalists. This 2017-2020 project is a) to create a pool o
f investigative journalists skilled in the use of multi-media and data journalism tools b) to spread that know-how to the regional media and strengthen their investigative journalism content. Nepal's media landscape is diversifying with the arrival of new 
media platforms including online news portals and social media providing an effective alternate medium. To stay effective and relevant in this changing landscape investigative reporters need to adapt to and adopt these skills. The former project 2013-2015 
taught basic skills in investigative journalism to 70 journalists in various regions of the country. The shifting eyeballs towards digital portals in the past years has shown Nepalis now have an alternative outlet of publication and syndication of investig
ative stories that would otherwise have been sensitive for the corporate media. However the use of digital interfaces that use multimedia and datajournalism tools is deficient. The evaluation report of the earlier project between CIJ and VIKES has stated n
ecessity for a training based on feedback from trainee reporterstrainers and editors working in various Nepali media. Accordingly the proposed project aims at training journalists to adopt digital tools for investigating and reporting on matters crucial to
 common interest. The project will also support CIJ to develop its own capacity to train and mentor investigative journalists in the use of multimedia and datajournalism techniques. Democratic ownership of development is a crucial element in human rights -
based development. For citizens to know their rights and be able to exercise them they need an independent and critical media that serves their right to information. This is effectively being done in developed countries through promotion of investigative j
ournalism. The project aims to do the same in Nepal through its activities.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Rural Village Water Resources Management Project in Nepal Second Phase
rows.description:
The overall objective of RVWRMP is institutionalised capacity at local and regional levels to sustain and continuously improve enhanced quality of life better environmental conditions and increased opportunities in rural livelihoods in the project area. Th
e purpose of the phase II is to achieve improved well-being and reduced poverty in project VDCs. The new phase emphasises the impacts of the project instead of means.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Strengthening Child Rights Monitoring System through Civil Society Organizations in Nepal
rows.description:
CWISH and Interpedia have cooperated to create a foundation among civil society organizations in Nepal to monitor the situation of child rights and strengthen the government mechanism to be accountable in protecting child rights. Through the cooperation th
e National Child Protection Alliance (NCPA) a confederation of 37 CSOs was formed. In 2015-2017 CWISH and Interpedia are working in 35 districts to capacitate 85 CSO members. The proposed 2017-2020 project is designed as a supplementary to current cooperat
ion to extend membership of NCPA to all 75 districts. In addition it seeks to mobilize 50 CSOs in at least 65 districts to monitor the child rights situation in Nepal through a new monitoring system.In the proposed project CSOs will utilize an online softw
are database system and mobile application through which 50 member organizations in 65 districts receive record respond refer and report cases of child rights violations to the government. Member organizations will follow up with the state mechanism for re
sponding to cases. CWISH will have a centralized database for the system and produce the Child Rights Status Country Report as well as segregated district-level reports providing recommendations to the government for formulating contextual and relevant pol
icies and programs. This will also reflect the situation of child rights in Nepal to the United Nations Conventions on the Rights of the Child and Universal Periodic Review in 2019. It is mandatory for Nepal to send a status report to UNCRC as a member sta
te ratifying the convention. Through the new system CSOs can produce a supplementary report with a civil society perspective.Extension of NCPA to all districts and their capacity development of CSOs offers an opportunity to consolidate information on the s
ituation of children throughout the country and help to prepare a complete map on the status of child rights in Nepal. Further capacitated government and strong civil society will bring a balance in respect to child rights act as an accountability tool for
 government and facilitate to provide legal and social justice to victims of rights violations.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: No Child Left Behind
rows.description:
The communities in the region are poor, marginalised and politically unstable. Education and skill levels are low and child labour and child marriages are common. School dropout-count is high. The earthquake of 2014 caused massive infrastructural destructi
on in some areas (e.g. schools were destroyed) and significantly weakened the income of families. Education can bring hope and open possibilities for children, youth and the communities. The project has improved 19 state schools’ teaching by providing su
pport for teaching, materials and teaching facilities. The project has supported youth through vocational course centres’ training. Especially challenged communities’ girls have been given preparing education, which enables a later transition to a norm
al primary school. Equivalent work will continue in 2016. The project is expected to improve vulnerable children’s, youth’s and communities’ quality of life. The purpose is to increase the knowledge and skill levels of communities, where the project 
acts (in Bhaktapur, Sarlah and Rukum). The project results in children’s improved primary education and youth’s vocational skills in the project area. Beneficiaries of the project are children, youth, their parents and teachers. Fida partners with a lo
cal NGO, Transformation Nepal (TFN).

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Coordination of Development Cooperation
rows.description:
The regional Development Manager contributes to ensure that the project planning, monitoring, evaluation and reporting complies with requirements. The regional Development Manager supports the local partners in project management and has an advisory functi
on in developing the quality of the projects. The regional Development Manager provides regularly information on project processes, local working context, and feedback from the local partners and the support they need, to the Development Cooperation Unit i
n Helsinki.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Concern for Education - Promoting Community Engagement (CFE-III)
rows.description:
Interpedia ry and Loo Niva Child Concern have previously partnered in Far West Nepal with projects in 2012-2014 and 2015-2017. The Concern for Education -projects have supported children?s right to access to free quality education in rural Far-West Dadeldh
ura area. Dadeldhura is one of the less developed areas in Nepal where vulnerable groups such as girls ethnic minorities and Dalits (the lower caste) still face discrimination and have lower accessibility to education. The projects have successfully manage
d to improve the status of basic education by training and sensitizing children parents teacher and local authorities for their role in promoting the Right to Education; by establishing children?s groups for strengthening children?s active participation in
 society; supporting local Concern for Education- and Education Watch Groups in supervising and promoting quality education and using local and social medium for raising awareness about education and good educational practices. The projects have aimed at m
aking schools as No Politics Zones supporting peaceceful education fulfillment of child rights and avoiding misuse of schools as an instrument of political targets and conflicts.For ensuring that the good practices that have been developed and introduced d
uring the previous phase can be spread the next phase of the project will focus even more on promoting basic and primary level education in Dadeldhura area with effective engagement of local actors of civil society. After the new constitution in September 
2015 NGOs and civil society have a significant opportunity to strengthen their role in policy-making as well as to advance the democratization process in Nepal. Due to that training and empowering local communities will be important parts of promoting educ
ation during forthcoming years. Trained local communities will also ensure sustainable redistribution of roles and responsibilities of improving the right to education as well as new ways to finance education promotion activities after the inputs from Loo 
Niva. The project balances direct project interventions and advocacy work with a clear geographicacal focus in the remote Far West area.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Developing a community mental health model in Nepal
rows.description:
The planned project is a continuation for the ongoing project ?Integrating Mental Health in Primary Health Care in a Post-conflict Setting 2012-2014? which took place in Dang district in Nepal. In the second phase of the project the aim is to improve equal
 access to effective treatment of mental health problems. The project will be expanded to cover the whole district (548.000 inhabitants). After this continuation phase responsibility of the project will be handed over to local authorities. The prevalence o
f mental health problems is high but no mental health professionals work in the public healthcare system in the whole district. The aim is to develop the activities implemented in the first phase of the project in a way that enables the actors of the local
 health system to continue them independently. All government health facilities including primary care units and hospitals will be covered.The immediate objectives are to improve the capacity of the primary health care workers to detect and treat common me
ntal disorders to improve access to specialised psychiatric services at district level and to raise the level of awareness of mental health and to reduce negative attitudes of the general population. Training sessions will be organised for female health ca
re volunteers primary health care workers and hospital-based medical officers. Four psychosocial counsellors hired by the project will provide counselling services as well as supervise trained primary health care workers in providing support to people who 
suffer from common mental disorders. The effectiveness of counselling will be studied by using a randomised controlled trial (RCT) design. Specialised care will be offered to a selected group of patients with severe disorders. Information about mental heal
th mental health problems and coping skills will be distributed via community mental health promotion workshops (CMHP) school mental health programmes radio programs and printed materials. Advocacy work will be focused on increasing the human resources in 
public mental health services in Dang.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: NEP/Multi Stakeholder Forest Sector Program in Nepal
rows.description:
The Swiss Agency for Development and Cooperation (SDC) acting through the Embassy of Switzerland has a lead role in the implementation of the Programme during 2011-2015. DFID and Finland have delegated the implementation of a transitional phase 2013-2015 t
o Switzerland. Due to slow start of the program it is envisaged that there will be a no-cost extension period for one year.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Technical Assistance for TEVT (Soft Skills) Development in School Sector Reform Plan in Nepal
rows.description:
The Government of Finland has contributed to development in the education sector since 1999 and is currently one of the development partners supporting the Nepali Government with financing and implementation of the School Sector Reform Plan (SSRP). Finland
?s contribution to the SSRP is ? 16 million during 2010 to 2015. Out of this ? 11.5 million has been allocated to finance the integration of Technical Vocational Education Training (TVET) into primary and secondary education. Additional to that there is an
 allocation of ? 1.6 million for development and piloting TEVT soft skills in school education.The overall objective is to contribute to enhancing quality and relevance of general education by introducing TEVT soft skills (vocationally oriented skills) in 
school system in order to improve employability of students and to meet the demands of labor market. The objective has been discussed with all major stakeholders and has been found to be relevant. No changes are required to the overall objective. The purpo
se of the Technical Assistance is to support the Ministry of Education in implementing the TEVT (Soft Skills) component of the SSRP that is operationalized in Annual Strategic Implementation Plans (ASIP) of each year.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Water, Sanitation and Hygiene Project (WASH)
rows.description:
Poor hygiene and sanitation, and unsafe drinking water are common problems in the Far-West development region, which is the poorest region in Nepal. Water and sanitation hygiene project is planned to improve the situation in project area with better sanita
tion, safe drinking water and healthy habits. In 2015, project has supported 262 low cost latrines and 92 hand pumps for clean drinking water facilities to poor and marginalized families. The goal of the project is improved quality of life among vulnerable
 persons and communities as result of strengthened partners. The purpose is improved water, sanitation, hygiene (WASH) and nutrition situation and practices in target communities in 5 VDCs of Kailali District. The project benefits women, children and other
 most vulnerable groups who become healthier after receiving training on good sanitation, personal hygiene, safe drinking water and nutrition. Fida partners with a local NGO, New Life Service Association (NSA) in the project.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Decent Work and Healthy Environment
rows.description:
The project develops Nepali forestry sector workers awareness, skills and methods in sustainable forest management. At the same time the trade unions capacity and joint action are promoted, and union membership enhanced.Often times the methods used in Nepa
li forest management have been short-sighted and the occupational health and safety (OHS) practices weak. In addition, the workers awareness and opportunities to effect on their terms has been minimal. Due to low organising rates unions have not been able 
to effectively defend workers interests. The project strengthens unions and OHS through education, training and organizing, contributes toward applying more sustainable forest management methods and forests viability and sustainable use. Increasingly union
s, with other civil society actors, will influence forest and conservation related policies, so that labour rights are taken into account in them.Union’s capacities, opportunities and networks will strengthen, and this will ensure more effective defendin
g of the workers’ rights application. Gradually, also collective agreeing and social dialogue will begin to improve positively toward workers terms and conditions. OHS situation will improve and finally the status of forests will become healthier and mul
tiple use of forests will realize.In the outset project’s beneficiaries are trade unions (skills, image, strength), which will gradually be better able to defend and promote worker’s rights, terms and OHS. Gradually, many forest and nature sector worke
rs will benefit from the project. Project is coordinated by Building and Wood Workers International Nepalese Affiliates Committee (BWI NAC), and implemented by their affiliates in Nepal (CAWUN and CUPPEC).

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Rural Water Supply and Sanitation Project in Western Nepal Completion Phase
rows.description:
Finland has in 2012 agreed to support Rural Water Supply and Sanitation Project in Western Nepal (RWSSP-WN II) with 137 million euros. The overall objective of the project is improved health and fulfilment of the equal right to water and sanitation service
s for the inhabitants of the Project area. In the last three years the Project has built 172 water supply schemes which serve approximately 85 000 people. From the 14 districts in the Project area only 3 are still without access to sanitation services. Fin
land will support the Project with an additional 1 million euro funding. The additional funding will increase the number of beneficiaries of the Project and guarantee the sustainability of the water supply schemes. With the additional funding (1 million eu
ros from Finland and 1 million euros from Nepal) the overall budget of the Project for the years 2013-2019 will be 2423 million euros of which Finland´s share will be 147 million euros.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Improving treatment care and rehabilitation services for people with spinal cord injury in Western Nepal
rows.description:
Spinal cord injury treatment care and rehabilitation remain major unmet needs in Nepal. Many cases of SCI are caused by falls from heights from trees (from which farmers cut fodder for their stock) and building sites. Road traffic accidents have become the
 leading cause of SCI in recent years. Moreover in 2015 two devastating earthquakes hit Nepal followed by hundreds of aftershocks. Estimates of injured vary between 22000-100000 many of them also having SCI.The International Nepal Fellowship (INF) owned Gr
een Pastures Hospital and Rehabilitation Centre (GPHRC) in Pokhara remains the only health facility outside Kathmandu Valley that is specialized in providing rehabilitation services to people with SCI. GPHRC provides services to the Western Development Reg
ion and through four INF clinics also to the Mid-Western Development Region - a total population of around 8.5 million people; in Pokhara and its surrounding areas 14 million. In the continuation phase the activities to improve rehabilitation services for 
people affected by SCI that were started in 2013-2016 project will partly remain the same. In addition GPHRC will start admitting SCI patients also in the acute phase straight after the trauma has happened. In many cases there is still a remarkable delay b
etween acute trauma care and beginning rehabilitation. Too often delay leads to secondary complications (esp. pressure sores) that could be avoided with appropriate care. The hospital aims at becoming a holistic treatment and care centre for SCI patients c
overing acute phase trauma treatment and care (incl. operative fixation) physical and mental rehabilitation education of patients and carers home assesments and modifications prior to discharge income generating activities for the injured follow-up and in 
some cases scholarships for the children of SCI patients to ensure that they can continue to go to school even after injury of the main bread winner of the family. The project will be implemented by International Nepal Fellowship Nepal?s (INF/Nepal) Green 
Pastures Hospital and Rehabilitation Centre (GPHRC) in collaboration with INF?s Community Based Rehabilitation (CBR) programme.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Institutional Cooperation between Finland and Nepal in meteorological services; 2nd Phase
rows.description:
The Finnish Meteorological Institute has carried out institutional cooperation (ICI) with the Nepalese counterpart Department of Hydrology and Meteorology (DHM) in the years 2010-2012. The second phase of the ICI-cooperation is carried out during 2013-2015
 with a total budget of 493 479 EUR. The main aim of the project is to strengthen the capacity of the DHM to make forecasts and provide weather services. DHM is an organization under the Ministry of Science Technology and Environment in Nepal. DHM has a ma
ndate from Government to monitor all the hydrological and meteorological activities in Nepal. The scope of work includes the monitoring of river hydrology climate agrometeorology sediment air quality water quality limnology snow hydrology glaciology and wi
nd and solar energy. General and aviation weather forecasts are the regular services provided by DHM. As a member of the World Meteorological Organisation (WMO) DHM contributes to the global exchange of meteorological data on a regular basis. The second ph
ase of the Finnish-Nepalese cooperation project will continue to respond to the development needs of DHM. The main technology and service areas of DHM development are: data management introducing new advanced technology improvement of weather forecasting (
3-day forecasts) and enhancing services for the society. All of these areas need capacity building. The project will support improvement of the DHM´s capacity to plan and develop hydrological and meteorological observation networks data management utiliza
tion of hydrometeorological data and provision of hydrometeorological services to the community and the industry. This will benefit all citizens of Nepal equally.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: UNDP/Strengthening the Rule of Law and Human Rights Protection System in Nepal
rows.description:
UNDP has prepared in cooperation with Supreme Court of Nepal Ministry of Law Justice Constituent Assembly and Parliamentary Affairs and National Human Rights Commission a programme titled ?Strengthening the Rule of Law and Human Rights Protection System in
 Nepal?. The UNDP led program is planned to be an multi-donor program for a five year period. The project basically aims to strengthen the rule of law by supporting firstly the ?administration of justice sector? with the Supreme Court playing a lead role t
hrough JSCC Secretariat and its network. Secondly through reforms in ?socio-legal? aspect of the rule of law and ?access to justice sector? the project envisages to implement protective laws by developing a ?national legal aid scheme? .?Access to justice? 
with focus on vulnerable groups needs special attention and has been positively designed to implement through ?legal aid centers? and ?legal aid service providers?. Affirmative programs with specified figures of the beneficiaries on the other hand have bee
n proposed to increase the number of hitherto deprived groups build their competency through educational scholarship internship and apprenticeship. Suggested legislative reforms to reduce discriminatory treatments and opportunities would have both symbolic
 value as well as impelling factor to gear up the project focus towards the needy groups. Total budget estimate for 5 years is USD 24 million of which Finland contributes 54 million euros during 2014-2017.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Nawalparasi and Palpa Districts Sustainable Water Supply and Sanitation Project (NAPA WASH)
rows.description:
The proposed project NAPA WASH is a continuation of WaterFinns involvement into WASH sector development in Nepal. It aims to complement the past and ongoing water sector support of Finland to Nepal. The Project Purpose is two-fold:1. Improved well-being an
d reduced poverty in Project areas2. Enhanced understanding of long-term sustainability of WASH servicesThe Project aims to achieve its Purpose through three interlinked results (outputs):Result 1: Lessons learned from success and failure of long-term sust
ainability of rural WASH services reported and benchmarks of best practices promotedResult 2: Capacity building of selected UC?s and VDC?s in their management and efforts of further improving the WASH servicesResult 3: Strengthened institutional structure 
to support communities and UC?s in O+M and Management of WASH services focusing on private sector service provision.One very important element of the proposed project is to create opportunities for the future water sector professionals (both Nepali and Fin
nish) to have a very interesting possibility to participate to monitoring related work that focuses on long-term learning of the sustainable schemes.The proposed project is three years long (2014-2016) and its proposed budget is 270000 EUR. The project geo
graphic area is Nawalparasi and Palpa Districts.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Education Governance - Promoting Good Governance in the Rural Villages of Lalitpur (EGP)
rows.description:
Although Nepal has made tremendous progress on educational attainment lately the hard to reach group children in the project location villages of Gimdi Asrang and Thuladurlung of Lalitpur district are out of school the drop-out rate is high and the quality
 of education and school management is not satisfactory. Effective management of school and education is seriously problematic highly centralized and with inadequate local ownership. Besides many community schools have become hotbeds of corruption with loc
al leaders fighting for the appointment of teachers and resources. The overall development objective is to attain improved status of education and literacy in project locations through efficient education governance in active engagement of community local 
government and participation of children. Immediate objectives of the project are enhanced engagement of local governments in education planning and interventions; improved access to school management and quality of education with capacitated accountable a
nd democratic School Management Committees (SMC); and increased engagement of parents community and children in educational planning and implementation.The project will enhance the right to quality education for easily marginalized groups such as dalit and
 tamang (or janajati) children. Inclusive education is promoted to children with disabilities. These actions contribute also to reduction of inequalities. Majority of the target group children are girls and therefore special attention is paid to consider g
ender equality in all activities. Climate sustainability is also taken into account in awareness raising campaigns conducted by child clubs.The project has a comprehensive target group who are key actors on changing school and education environment. It inc
ludes the 4100 target children and their parents policy makers civil society actors media schoolteachers SMCs Parents Teachers Association (PTA) members and education officials. These groups are reached through different interventions; such as capacity bui
lding collaborative consultations with responsible authorities along with wider awareness campaigns and mass mobilizations. The project will be implemented in collaboration of Department of Education Ministry of Federal and Local Governance respective loca
l governments and District Education Offices and other NGO coalitions active in the sector.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: FLC: NP/FLC OLE Nepal Improving Access to Quality Basic Education through ICT-integrated Classes Phase II
rows.description:
Improving Access to Quality Basic Education through ICT-integrated Classes- Improve the quality of education in the public education system through the use of ICT resources modern pedagogical teaching-learning techniques and integrated teaching methods. - 
Mobilize participation from stakeholders of communities to ensure improved quality of education of public schools. - Build capacities of public school teachers district level resources and central level planners policy makers and implementers within the Mi
nistry of Education?s various line agencies. - Support the Ministry of Education in its effort to integrate ICT in education. - Design develop and distribute a comprehensive set of curriculum-based digital learning activities that help Nepali children ?lea
rn learning?. - Build maintain and distribute a digital library consisting of education resources.Implementation of ICT-integrated child-centric teaching/learning method in 15 public schools using laptops shared by various classes; Capacity building of tea
chers to use the digital learning activities to teach English Mathematics and Science to Classes 7-8 students; Capacity building of Resource Centers and District Education Offices on monitoring and support.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Child Sensitive Social Protection
rows.description:
The project reduces child poverty and the use of child labor. It increases the number of children regularly attending school and improves child well-being in Sindhupalchowk and Kavre Districts. Children below 18 years of age constitute 42 % of the total po
pulation of Nepal and more than a third of Nepalese children live below the national poverty line. Many of them suffer from severe malnutrition. Around 10 % of children do not have access to schooling and about 40 % of children between 5-17 years of age ar
e working. The project improves access to social protection schemes by children and poor households. As a result of this project, children’s nutritional status improves and an increasing number of them attend school. The beneficiaries of this project are
 the most vulnerable children and their families. The project is implemented by Save the Children Nepal, Tuki Sangh Sunkoshi (Sindhupalchowk) and Nangshal Association Nepal (Kavre).

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Creating protective Environment for Children

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Promotion of Women’s Economic, Social and Cultural Rights in Lalitpur
rows.description:
Poor women from deprived communities are not in a position to access loan from banks or other financial institutions or financial service providers due to lack of physical collateral. The poor, indigenous women and girls are exploited from various forms of
 discrimination. Therefore there is a comprehensive need to build the capacity of deprived poor women to increase their access to financial capital with low interest rate. The local government bodies have not fully owned and realised the importance of wome
n’s participation in planning process and providing justifiable share of resources to the women in their location. Strengthening cooperatives and their linkages with local bodies, including Ward Citizen Forum and Citizen Awareness Centre (CAC) are requir
ed to increase women’s access to resources and services. Most of the women still lack legal ownership of their land. Cooperatives still lack adequate knowledge and skills to develop strong linkages with market actors for enterprise development. Lack of v
ocational skill training and micro entrepreneurship skills among women and out of school youth girls, denial of women to property rights, gender based discrimination and women’s right violation justify the need to implement this project in Lalitpur distr
ict of Nepal. The overall objective of the project is to improve women’s economic and socio-cultural status. The specific objective is to develop and strengthen women-led local institutions for promoting economic, social and cultural rights of poor and m
arginalised women communities in Lalitpur. Expected results:• Increased women’s access to and control over land and capital• Increased income through employment opportunities for women and girls• Improved women’s economic, social and cultural rig
hts through enhancement of capacities of local government bodies and community organisationsRights-holders /beneficiaries:  5 000 women and girls mainly from the poor indigenous and Dalits communitiesImplementing partner: The Lutheran World Federation Nepa
l (LWF Nepal).

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Skills training and Technical Education for Employment Programme
rows.description:
There is a huge gap between the demand and supply of skilled man power in domestic market. The lack of employment opportunities, insufficient vocational skills training opportunities are pushing unemployed youths for out-migration. They get low remuneratio
n and are prone to accidents as they are mostly engaged in Danger, Difficult and Dirty (3D) jobs. Therefore, these unemployed rural youths need to be trained on emerging trades to sharpen their skills for their earnings and livelihoods. This will contribut
e to increased remunerations and job security. New opportunities have been waiting for the skilled youths within their own districts, regional markets, Indian markets as well as abroad markets.The project aims at enhancing access of women and youths to qua
lity vocational skill training and technical education by strengthening technical vocational education and training (TVET) sub-sector in Nepal. Expected results:• Enhanced access of women and youths to quality TVET service• TVET sub–sector knowledge 
and experiences are systematically documented and widely disseminated among the concerned• Strengthened capacity of public and private TVET service providers to deliver quality serviceRights-holders /beneficiaries: Unemployed women and youth Implementing
 partner:  Underprivileged Children’s Education Programs (UCEP Nepal)

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Strengthening Diversity to Promote Unity for Strong Trade Union Movement
rows.description:
This project will help to find out the current situation of CBAs and its implementations to support joint campaigning on issues like minimum wages and other issues for better industrial relation. It also helps to advocate and lobby for a worker friendly co
nstitution. At the same time, it will build the capacity of the 3 ITUC affiliates in key areas where they are weak in their organizations. The fundamental idea is to support the joint initiatives of the trade union movement in Nepal to promote permanent pe
ace from the workers side, while also simultaneously addressing the core weakness of the 3 national centers which play a key role in the JTUCC. Nepal trade union movement is fairly young and fragmented into 11 central unions, which are typically aligned wi
th political parties. In order to be effectively able to influence constitution drafting process, minimum wages setting and social security, the movement must act in cooperation on joint agenda. This is made possible through JTUCC and also each union’s c
apacities will be enhanced.The minimum wage setting happens in every second year in Nepal. This project will help trade unions make informed decisions and plans re minimum wage campaign and advocacy work on the same. Each union’s weaknesses will be enhan
ced through training.The beneficiaries are each central union, their officers and leaders, and in longer run all Nepalese workers.Implementing partners are central unions through their joint platform JTUCC, and the contracting partner will be ITUC NAC, as 
all three unions are ITUC affiliates.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Free Legal Service and Mobile Clinic for Poor and Marginalised Women and Children
rows.description:
Women are often victims of the discrimination. Living in remote rural areas, women's rights are often unsatisfactory. The reasons for the unequal situation can be found in cultural, economic and political factors. Nepal's judicial system is relatively slow
 and inefficient. Women's access to legal institutions is generally still weaker than for men and women are often not aware of their rights. The beneficiaries are the most vulnerable women and in need of legal assistance and counseling in Kathmandu, Kask, 
and Banken counties. The project also aims to strengthen the role of civil society in promoting women's rights in by for example networking and information-collection. Legal Aid and Consultancy Centre (LACC) was founded by women lawyers, and its goal is to
 get active women players in Nepal's social, legal and economic development. At the same time the organization, aims at protecting and promoting on women's and children's rights. Legal Aid and Consultancy Centre (LACC) was founded by women lawyers, and its
 goal is to get active women players in Nepal's social, legal and economic development. At the same time the organization, aims at protecting and promoting on women's and children's rights http://lacc.org.np/

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Centre for Victims of Torture
rows.description:
The project trains people who work with women suffered from violence. The aim is to train doctors, lawyers, police officers, and human rights activists, so that they can better work to promote rights of the women, victims of violence. Violence against wome
n in Nepal is a widespread problem, to which the authorities do not interfere with the necessary severity - at least on a practical level. For example, Amnesty International recently reported that the police authorities in cases of violence against women t
ake more the side of the guilty than the victim's side. Domestic violence is seen as a private matter for families and abused women rarely even seek help for the known obstacles. Lawyers often do not like to assume responsibility for cases of violence agai
nst women-related, and when the evidence has not been adequately documented, the victims are virtually impossible to get justice. The main objective is to promote rights of the women, victims of violence. The aim is to guarantee access to the relevant serv
ices, and to increase the capacity service providers and the authorities, so that women's rights are better met. The beneficiaries are the authorities participating in the project. The project also offers support to 50 women suffered from violence and info
rms women about the services for victims of violences. The project is implemented in the Kavre county. CVICT was founded and registered as an NGO in 1990 and was originally used by volunteers for Victims of Torture in Kathmandu. Organization's founders wer
e doctors, social workers and nurses. CVICTin focuses on increasing rehabilitation of torture victims and prevention of torture. Legal aid has long been a part of the CVICTin the support of victims of torture. www.cvict.org.np

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Sensitizing Responsible Authorities Being Responsive Towards the Education, Food and Health Issues of Local People - jatkohanke
rows.description:
The Government has taken a number of action plans and policies designed to promote the realization of public health, education and food security. At the local level policies and plans rarely are implemented, and examples of the money allocated to the vulne
rable groups' education often ends up in some other use. The project aims to promote ESC rights, especially the the realization right to education, food and health. The project aims to promote that the Government's plans for the realization of the above-me
ntioned rights are concerned, to increase the public officers’ responsibility and capacity, by increasing the transparency of public authorities and the beneficiaries' awareness of their rights. The project builds the capacity of the responsible authorit
ies, so that they contribute to the realization of plans the government the right to education, food and health-related issues. The project will also inform the communities about their rights focusing on these rights. The project will be implemented in fiv
e counties: Morang, Makawanpur, Kaski, Banke and Kailali. INSEC is Nepal's most important and most reliable universal human rights organizations. Informal Sector Service Centre INSEC was registered in 1991. Organization works particularly strong in documen
tation and reporting of human rights and is a reliable information source of human rights situation in Nepal. www.inseconline.org

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Nepal country programme
rows.description:
Nepal’s biodiversity is under constant threat from habitat degradation and fragmentation, deforestation and forest degradation which are driven by unplanned infrastructure development, uncontrolled forest fires. The iconic Himalayan species as tigers and
 snow leopards are threaten by poaching and illegal wildlife trade as well as causing human wildlife conflicts in some areas. Moreover, climate change has exacerbated the threats to biodiversity and people’s livelihoods.We work in two priority landscapes
 in Nepal: Terai Arc Landscape in Southern-Nepal and Sacred Himalayan Landscape in North-East-Nepal. The conservation work improves corridor and buffer zone conservation and management, reduces deforestation and strengthens community-based conservation. Ou
r work enables increase in the population of key species such as rhino and tiger by decreased illegal wildlife trade and poaching and protection of their habitats. Improvement of the livelihoods and wellbeing of the local communities is gained through deve
loping income generating activities such as forest-based enterprises and by increasing access to energy and developing health and hygiene solutions. In addition we increase conservation awareness and empower local youth to act for the environment. We also 
develop integrated river basin management and water smart communities which help local communities to adapt in climate change. Beneficiaries: the local community forest user groups, women and youth groups, ethnic minority groups, forest workers and disadva
ntaged groups. Implementor: WWF Nepal: http://www.wwfnepal.org/, More information about our interventions: http://www.wwfnepal.org/what_we_do/special_projects/decent_work__healthy_environment/expected_results/, Generation Green: http://tgg.org.np/, Buildin
g and Wood Worker's International - Nepal Affiliates Committee: https://www.facebook.com/bwi.nac

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Improving school environment in Nepal
rows.description:
In rural Nepal especially girls encounter challenges related to schooling, one of them being lack of toilets suitable for girls. There is also need for improvement in teachers' attitudes, transparency of school administration, infrastructure and safe envir
onment. With closer cooperation betweeen different school stakeholdersthe quality of schools can be improved and the attitudes in communities can be changed to understand the importance of schooling in children's life. The project aims to develop child and
 environmentally friendly schools in rural Eastern Nepal by supporting school child clubs in their activities and the cooperation between school stakeholders (child club, school management committee, parent-teacher assotiation, school administration, Villa
ge education committee) in preparing school improvement plans (SIP). The project provides capacitation to Child Club board members on child rights, life skills, gender, social inclusion and environmental issues. The school stakeholders participate in capac
itations and workshops to manage the schools efficiently and transparently. Through Child Clubs the youth can contribute to the development of their own shools and communities. There is also hardware components to improve the classrooms, sanitation and saf
e drinking water facilities in project schools. The project will result quality rural schools with good environment to study and work.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Rural Village Water Resources Management Project (III phase)
rows.description:
The Rural Village Water Resources Management Project in Nepal Phase II (2010-2015) has after four years of implementation achieved better water supply for 93 000 people sanitation for 269 000 people and home gardens for 155 000 people. The purpose of phase
 III is to achieve full coverage of water supply and sanitation and establishment of functional planning and implementation frameworks for all water uses in nine project districts. The expected results of the project are: 1) institutionalized community cap
acity to construct and maintain community managed water supply and adopt appropriate technologies and behavior 2) Improved and sustainable nutrition food security and sustainable income at community level through water resources based livelihoods developme
nt 3) Institutionalized capacity at district level to continue integrated water resources planning and support to communities in implementing and maintaining WASH and livelihood activities. In terms of beneficiaries the project will reach over 300 000 peop
le. The executing agency is Ministry of Federal Affairs and Local Development of Nepal together with participating District Development Committees (DDC). Finland will support the project with 15 million euros from 2015 to 2020.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Improving sexual and reproductive health and rights and biodiversity conservation through integrated approach
rows.description:
The development problem of the proposed project is limited access to SRHR combined with unsustainable use of natural resources. The purpose of the project is to improve SRHR and biodiversity conservation through integrated approach. The project focuses on 
peoples empowerment capacity building and awareness raising improving livelihood and well-being as well as strengthening partnerships to promote an integrated approach. Capacity building and awareness raising activities will be targeted towards young peopl
e and Natural Resource Management communities. Through the trainings it is expected that their understanding of the issues and motivation to promote sustainable development and SRHR are increased. The long-term development objective for the integrated Popu
lation Health and Environment (PHE) initiative is Healthy and productive communities where young people can live in harmony with nature and realize their SRHR. This project is a continuation project and forms a third phase of a collaborative PHE project wh
ich is implemented through an integrated approach with four partners: Väestöliitto (the Family Federation of Finland) FPAN (the Family Planning Association of Nepal) WWF Finland and WWF Nepal. Cooperation between these partners started already in 2011 an
d this project builds upon the lessons learned from the first two phases of the project. The project will be directly linked to the PHE initiative of WWF Nepal and WWF Finland supported by the MFA. These two projects will complement each other and there wi
ll be close partnership and coordination with WWF Nepal at the central and field levels.Capacity building and awareness raising activities will be targeted towards young people and Natural Resource Management (NRM) communities.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Preventive Education for Uterine Prolapse in Nepal
rows.description:
In Nepal there are about 14 million women with Uterine prolapse. Though the expected lifetime has become longer in Nepal the amount of women who get uterine prolapse has not decreased. It has doubled from the year 2008. The problem is in the Nepalese cultu
re. It does not protect the the women after they given a birth to a child. Many of the women are expected to work hard soon after the delivery.The aim of this project is to secure a better health for all in Nepal and to prevent Uterine prolapse- by support
ing them to appreciate the motherhood and to protect the mothers after the delivery - by counselling in breast-feeding and in child care- by teaching right ways to lift and to work - by guiding to healthy living habits and versatile diet- by encouraging to
 smoke free lifestyle with no intoxicating agents or fear of AIDSIt is planned that the project will reach all the 75 districts in Nepal in three years. Partners in the districts will be the health and education departments of the districts and also the vo
luntary women workers groups which already exist in the districts. According to the plan in each district a three day seminar will be arranged in which all the health teachers from the schools health assistants midwives and the voluntary women workers in t
he district will be invited. Each year about 2500 health workers can take part in the seminar (100 in each district). All these will get the health education teaching material used during the seminar so that they can use it also in their own work. So the a
mount of the Nepalese reached by the project will be several time larger.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: UN Women - Women´s Economic Empowerment
rows.description:
UN Women´s Women´s Economic Empowerment project aims to enhance the economic situation of especially the most poorest groups of Nepalese women. The participation of Nepalese women in the advancement of the national economy is limited due to insufficient 
economic resources and low level of education. The project advances the consciousness of the beneficiaries regarding their rights through networks of marginalized women. The project builds up the capacity of the Government of Nepal to follow the conditions
 of equality in its economic policy both at national and provincial level. Specific beneficiaries can be named female migrant workers domestic servants HIV positive women victims of conflicts and human trafficking and the female farmers of remote areas. UN
 Women´s office in Nepal will execute the project and contract will be concluded with the main office of UN Women. Finland will support the project with 4 000 000 euros from 2015 to 2017.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Technical Assistance for Competence-Based Soft Skills Development in School Education in Nepal
rows.description:
The Technical Assistance for Competence-Based Soft Skills Development in School Education in Nepal (TASS) is the second phase of the Finnish funded TSSP program which was implemented in the years 2012-2015. The aim of the TSSP program was to improve the qu
ality of education in Nepal by integrating soft skills into teaching. Soft skills are defined as personal cooperation thinking innovation and business skills. The TSSP project ends in December 2015.The mid-term review of TSSP concluded that the integration
 of soft skills into the education sector in Nepal is highly relevant as it can improve both the quality and the relevance of education. The evaluation recommended a continuation of the project. The main achievements of TSSP were the approval of new curric
ula in six different subjects for grades 9-10 as well as the development of teachers training materials and their testing. The new textbooks for grade 9 will be in schools in 2016 and teachers' training packages have been taught in 40% of Nepal's teacher t
raining facilities. The second phase ? the TASS project ? will build on the achievements of the first phase. During the TASS program the update of the curriculum for grades 9-10 moves to the implementation stage. Teaching materials are being developed also
 for grades 6-8 and work on materials for grades 9-10 continues. The education of teachers and capacity building of relevant institutions regarding the application of soft skills also continues. The beneficiaries of the project are Nepalese students in gra
des 6-10. The immediate beneficiaries are the Ministry of Education and regional and district level education directorates and offices. In addition the projects 80 pilot schools and their teachers benefit from focused capacity building support. The Ministr
y of Education in Nepal implements the project. The proposed budget for the project is 17 MEUR for the years 2016-2018.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: UNICEF - WASH Earthquake Recovery Programme
rows.description:
The catastrophic earthquake of 25th April 2015 measuring 7.8 Richter scale had its devastating consequences on human lives and property as well as infrastructures including WASH services in Nepal. The gains that Nepal had made so far in achieving the MDG t
argets on water and sanitation has been negatively challenged by the damages of water supply and sanitation services. UNICEF WASH Earthquake Recovery Programme will support reconstruction efforts in Nepal particularly in WASH sector with a particular focus
 on Disaster Risk Management. The expected results and outcomes of the programme are formulation and operationalization of disaster risk reduction and climate change policies capacity building and system building for disaster preparedness and response and 
earthquake affected water supply schemes and school toilets and water systems rehabilitation. Rehabilitated water supply schemes will serve at least 33 000 people and school toilets and water systems rebuilding will serve at least 20 000 children. At least
 20 000 school children will be also educated on hand washing.In International Conference on Nepal?s Reconstruction on 25th of June 2015 Finland pledged two million euros (2 000 000) to the reconstruction efforts in Nepal. The contribution was decided to b
e channeled through UNICEF based on earlier cooperation. Finland provided one million euros (1 000 000) support for 2016. The programme will receive additional funding of one million euros for 2017 as pledged in 25th of June 2015.
