rows.reporting: United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO)
rows.title: Strengthening Press Freedom, Safety of Media Professionals and the Right to Access to Information Online and Offline in South Asia
rows.description: This project seeks to support the engagement of media stakeholders in South Asia in advocacy activities involving UNESCO member states with the objective of promoting Freedom of Expression, Right to Access to information online and offline and the promotion of the Safety of Media Professionals in South Asia. The activity will be implemented in line with the UN Plan of Action on the safety of Journalists and the Issue of Impunity and the ROAM principles. There will also be a strong focus on promoting gender and media.

rows.reporting: United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO)
rows.title: Building a gendered media which contributes to diversity, youth empowerment and emergency and disaster response in South Asia
rows.description: gender inequalities in human development, reveals a gap in South Asia that is larger than that of any other region. In 2014, South Asia scored 19.3 percent higher than the global average, indicating widespread discrimination. 60% of people aged between 15 and 24 live in Asia and the Pacific; that's about 700 million youth. Out of the 700 million youth, over 85 million young people in the region live in extreme poverty on less than $1.90 a day. This activity seeks to contribute to addressing the vulnerability of women and youth in South Asia through Media, for their strengthened contribution to Sustainable Development and a better environment of a Culture of peace in the region.

rows.reporting: United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO)
rows.title: Strengthening Universal Access to information and Open and inclusive solutions in ICTs for Sustainable Development in South Asia
rows.description: This activity seeks to promote policy interventions for the integration of ICT in Education in South Asia, a region where capacity and infrastructure to support ICT in Education in South Asia is still lacking. The project will also support safe use of cyberspace where the relevancy of information on the internet is increasingly being questioned, and the potential of cyberspace to be used for radicalisation of the youth and children is high. The project will also support member states to develop platforms of curated data and information sources such as digital libraries to promote quality and inclusive learning environments. Finally this activity will strengthen regional cooperation by establishing a regional networking platform.

rows.reporting: United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO)
rows.title: Enhancing the Memory of the World Programme in South Asia
rows.description: This activity will intervene in South Asia to ameliorate challenges in the area of identification, preservation, access to and dissemination of documentary heritage. The activity will support the establishment of inclusive and collaborative mechanisms, which involve member states and all partners to find solutions to the current challenges in the MoW programme. Through this activity for instance MoW national Committees/expert groups will be created, awareness raising programmes will be delivered and capacity development activities to MoW stakeholders will also be delivered.

rows.reporting: United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO)
rows.title: Strengthening national capacity to improve the protection, monitoring and sustainable management of the World Heritage properties, in particular, to help recover Nepal's cultural heritage affected by the 2015 earthquakes
rows.description: Nepal's aim to build back better by restoring the cultural heritage damaged by the 2015 earthquake will require sustained efforts, both in terms of capacity enhancement and improved procedures, tools, and mechanism in place. The Office's implementation modality will be, in line with the regional priorities of culture sector and Nepal's national priority, through policy advice, advocacy, fund-raising, coordination, capacity building programmes for government and non-governmental stakeholders and members of the local communities, both men and women, including youth and pilot projects, in particular at the country's four World Heritage properties, Lumbini the birthplace of Lord Buddha, the Kathmandu Valley, as well as the National Parks of Chitwan and Sagarmatha.
